title: Cynoglossum regelii
intro: Cynoglossum regelii, also known as Regel's hound's-tongue, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as the ability to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Cynoglossum regelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to alleviate various health conditions, including coughs and colds.
